2605.05103.title: Text Corpora as Concept Fields: Black-Box Hallucination and Novelty Measurement
2605.05103.paper_summary: We introduce the **Concept Field** of a text corpus: a local drift field with pointwise uncertainty, estimated in sentence-embedding space from the deltas between consecutive sentences. Given a candidate sentence transition, we score its agreement with the field by $ζ$, the mean absolute z-distance between the observed delta and the field's local Gaussian estimate. The score is black-box (no model internals), corpus-attributable (every score traces to nearby corpus sentences), and admits a direct probabilistic reading. We support the computation with the introduction of a **Vector Sequence Database (VSDB)** that stores embeddings together with sequence-position and next-delta metadata. We evaluate this approach on two large-scale settings: hallucination-style groundedness detection over the U.S. Code of Federal Regulations, and novelty detection over Project Gutenberg. Using controlled LLM-generated rewrites, Concept Fields achieve strong selective classification performance under a grounded / ungrounded / unsure triage policy, which unlike retrieval-centric baselines have similar coverage-risk behavior across both domains, supporting a probability-based interpretation that transfers across domains. We also sketch how divergence and curl of the Concept Field, computed on dense clusters, surface qualitatively meaningful semantic patterns (logic sources, sinks, and implicit topics), which we offer as hypothesis-generating rather than as a quantitative result. Concept Fields provide a fast, lightweight, and interpretable signal for groundedness and novelty, complementary to LLM-as-judge and white-box detectors.
2605.05103.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹文本語料庫的**概念場**：一個具有點狀不確定性的局部漂移場，從連續句子之間的變化中在句子嵌入空間中進行估計。給定一個候選句子轉換，我們通過$ζ$來評分其與場的協議，$ζ$是觀察到的變化與場的局部高斯估計之間的平均絕對z距離。這個分數是黑箱的（沒有模型內部），可歸因於語料庫（每個分數都可追溯到附近的語料庫句子），並且具有直接的概率解釋。我們通過引入**向量序列資料庫（VSDB）**來支持計算，該資料庫存儲嵌入以及序列位置和下一變化的元數據。我們在兩個大型設定上評估這種方法：在美國聯邦法規上進行的幻覺風格的基礎檢測，以及在古騰堡計劃上的新穎性檢測。使用控制的LLM生成重寫，概念場在基於基礎/非基礎/不確定的分流政策下實現了強大的選擇性分類性能，這與以檢索為中心的基準相比，在兩個領域中具有相似的覆蓋風險行為，支持跨領域轉移的基於概率的解釋。我們還勾勒出概念場的發散和旋度，這些是在密集集群上計算的，顯現出質量上有意義的語義模式（邏輯源、匯和隱含主題），我們將其作為生成假設而非定量結果。概念場提供了一個快速、輕量且可解釋的信號，用於基礎性和新穎性，補充了LLM作為評判者和白箱檢測器。
2605.05103.author: Nicholas S. Kersting et.al.
2605.05103.authors: Nicholas S. Kersting, Vittorio Castelli, Chieh Ting Yeh, Xinzhu Wang, Saad Taame
